verses.text: God bids them camp by the sea, that Pharaoh think them trapped, and He be honoured upon him. The lure. God draws Pharaoh to ruin for His glory.
God bids them camp by the sea, that Pharaoh think them trapped, and He be honoured upon him. The lure. God draws Pharaoh to ruin for His glory.
God bids them camp by the sea, that Pharaoh think them trapped, and He be honoured upon him. The lure. God draws Pharaoh to ruin for His glory.
God bids them camp by the sea, that Pharaoh think them trapped, and He be honoured upon him. The lure. God draws Pharaoh to ruin for His glory.
Pharaoh regrets letting them go and pursues with six hundred chosen chariots. Pursuit. The freed are chased by their old master.
Pharaoh regrets letting them go and pursues with six hundred chosen chariots. Pursuit. The freed are chased by their old master.
Pharaoh regrets letting them go and pursues with six hundred chosen chariots. Pursuit. The freed are chased by their old master.
He overtakes them encamped by the sea. Cornered. Egypt closes in behind them.
He overtakes them encamped by the sea. Cornered. Egypt closes in behind them.
Terrified, Israel cries out and blames Moses: 'better... serve the Egyptians.' Fear. Trouble makes them long for bondage.
Terrified, Israel cries out and blames Moses: 'better... serve the Egyptians.' Fear. Trouble makes them long for bondage.
Terrified, Israel cries out and blames Moses: 'better... serve the Egyptians.' Fear. Trouble makes them long for bondage.
Moses answers, 'Fear ye not, stand still, and see the salvation of the LORD.' Stand still. Deliverance is God's work, not theirs.
Moses answers, 'Fear ye not, stand still, and see the salvation of the LORD.' Stand still. Deliverance is God's work, not theirs.
God bids Moses lift his rod; He will divide the sea and gain honour over Pharaoh. The command. Faith moves forward at God's word.
God bids Moses lift his rod; He will divide the sea and gain honour over Pharaoh. The command. Faith moves forward at God's word.
God bids Moses lift his rod; He will divide the sea and gain honour over Pharaoh. The command. Faith moves forward at God's word.
God bids Moses lift his rod; He will divide the sea and gain honour over Pharaoh. The command. Faith moves forward at God's word.
The pillar moves behind them, dark to Egypt, light to Israel. Between. God Himself stands between His people and the foe.
The pillar moves behind them, dark to Egypt, light to Israel. Between. God Himself stands between His people and the foe.
Moses stretches his hand; the sea parts, and Israel walks through on dry ground. The path. God opens a way where there was none.
Moses stretches his hand; the sea parts, and Israel walks through on dry ground. The path. God opens a way where there was none.
The Egyptians follow; God troubles their host and takes off their chariot wheels. Bogged down. Egypt finds the Lord fights against them.
The Egyptians follow; God troubles their host and takes off their chariot wheels. Bogged down. Egypt finds the Lord fights against them.
The Egyptians follow; God troubles their host and takes off their chariot wheels. Bogged down. Egypt finds the Lord fights against them.
The sea returns and covers Pharaoh's whole army; 'there remained not so much as one.' Drowned. The pursuers perish utterly.
The sea returns and covers Pharaoh's whole army; 'there remained not so much as one.' Drowned. The pursuers perish utterly.
The sea returns and covers Pharaoh's whole army; 'there remained not so much as one.' Drowned. The pursuers perish utterly.
Israel sees the Egyptians dead, fears the LORD, and believes Him and His servant Moses. Saved. Deliverance brings reverence and faith.
